claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit! Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To start, I need to ask you some basic questions about your situation.

**First, let me ask about the child or children you're claiming for:**

How old is your child? (Or if you have multiple children, let's start with the first one)

The eligibility rules differ based on whether the child is:
- Under 16 years old
- Between 16-19 years old
- 20 years or older

Please tell me the age of the child you're asking about, and their name so I can keep track as we go through the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my son Alex. He's 6 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for that information. Let me assess Alex's eligibility.

Since Alex is 6 years old, he falls into the "under 16" category. Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news - at 6 years old, Alex automatically meets the age requirement for Child Benefit.

Now, the next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Since Alex is only 6 years old, I'm assuming he's not in paid employment, but I need to confirm this to continue with the assessment. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he's not working at all. He's only 6, so he's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, that makes sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now the next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in his own right?**

This means benefits like:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

For a 6-year-old in primary school, this would be very unusual. Can you confirm that Alex is not receiving any of these benefits in his own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits himself. He's just a normal 6-year-old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now the next check:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship program?**

Again, at 6 years old and in primary school, this wouldn't apply to Alex, but I need to confirm for the assessment. Can you confirm he's not in an apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not. He's only 6, so he's nowhere near that stage yet.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely right! Let me move forward.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Now we've established that Alex qualifies as a child. The next set of questions is about **you** as the claimant.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if ANY of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**, OR
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, OR
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3 months), OR
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (for up to 91 days), OR
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself, OR
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources, OR
7. You're a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside, OR
8. You're a Crown servant living abroad

**Do you meet any of these residency requirements?** The most common is simply that you normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't actually live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. That's an important detail. Let me ask some follow-up questions to see if you might still qualify under one of the special circumstances.

**Since you're living abroad, can you tell me:**

1. **Are you a Crown servant** (e.g., working for the UK government, diplomatic service, or armed forces abroad)?

2. **Or are you living in certain qualifying countries** where you can still claim Child Benefit?

If neither of these applies, do you have any connection to the EU Settlement Scheme (settled or pre-settled status)?

These are the main ways someone living outside the UK can still qualify for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I don't have any EU settlement status either. I'm just living abroad with my family.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. Based on what you've told me, let me navigate to the appropriate outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that based on the information you've provided, **you are not currently eligible for UK Child Benefit for Alex**.

**The reason:** You do not meet the UK residency requirements.

**Why this matters:** To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK or meet one of the special circumstances, such as:
- Being a Crown servant working abroad
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria)
- Living in certain qualifying countries

Since you're living abroad without any of these special circumstances, you don't meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit.

**What you could do:**
- If you return to live in the UK in the future, you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time (as Alex meets all the child-related requirements)
- If your circumstances change (for example, if you become a Crown servant or obtain relevant immigration status), you may become eligible

Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about this decision, or do you have any questions about what might make you eligible in the future?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old and meets all child-related eligibility criteria: he is under 16, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant, do not have EU settled/pre-settled status, and are not living in qualifying countries. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex at this time.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is living abroad with their family and does not meet any of the special circumstances that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while outside the UK. The child Alex meets all qualifying criteria, but the claimant's residency status makes them ineligible to claim.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old and meets all child-related eligibility criteria: he is under 16, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant, do not have EU settled/pre-settled status, and are not living in qualifying countries. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex at this time.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is living abroad with their family and does not meet any of the special circumstances that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while outside the UK. The child Alex meets all qualifying criteria, but the claimant's residency status makes them ineligible to claim.
